report_name: Aligned Orthopedic Partners Discloses Data Breach Exposing Extensive Patient and Financial Data

threat_actors.name: Unknown Actor
threat_actors.description: An unidentified threat actor who gained unauthorized access to the corporate email system of Aligned Orthopedic Partners for one month, from November 16, 2025, to December 16, 2025. The actor exfiltrated a large volume of patient PII and PHI.
threat_actors.goals: Exfiltration of sensitive patient data, including Social Security numbers, financial account details, and protected health information, likely for financial fraud or sale on the dark web.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: Identified as a likely initial access vector where an employee was tricked into revealing their credentials.

attack_patterns.name: Password Spraying
attack_patterns.description: Mentioned as a possible initial access method where the attacker used automated tools to guess common passwords for multiple employee accounts.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: The attacker used compromised employee email accounts to access the corporate email environment.

attack_patterns.name: Email Collection
attack_patterns.description: The primary activity of the threat actor after gaining access was to search for and exfiltrate sensitive data from the compromised email system.

attack_patterns.name: Local Email Collection
attack_patterns.description: The attacker searched through compromised mailboxes to find sensitive patient PII and PHI.

attack_patterns.name: Remote Email Collection
attack_patterns.description: The attacker may have set up forwarding rules to automatically exfiltrate incoming and outgoing emails to an external account.

identities.name: Aligned Orthopedic Partners
identities.description: A healthcare provider that suffered a data breach of its corporate email environment, exposing extensive patient PII and PHI.

identities.name: Cyberscout
identities.description: The identity protection services firm offered to individuals affected by the Aligned Orthopedic Partners data breach.

indicators.name: Suspicious Email Forwarding Rule Creation
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Email Forwarding Rule Creation
id: 2b8a1c9d-4e5f-4a1b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of a new inbox rule that forwards emails to an external domain, a common TTP for data exfiltration in a Business Email Compromise (BEC) scenario.
references:
  - https://claimdepot.com/blog/aligned-orthopedic-discloses-data-breach-affecting-social-security-numbers/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-19
modified: 2026-04-19
tags:
  - attack.t1114.002
  - attack.collection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate email forwarding rules configured by users or administrators.
level: medium
logsource:
  product: m365
  service: audit.exchange
detection:
  selection:
    Operation: 'New-InboxRule'
    Parameters|contains:
      - 'ForwardTo'
      - 'ForwardAsAttachmentTo'
  filter:
    UserId|contains: '@yourdomain.com' # Replace with your internal domain to filter out external forwarding
  condition: selection and not filter
indicators.description: Detects the creation of email inbox rules that forward messages externally, a common technique for data exfiltration in email compromise incidents like the one affecting Aligned Orthopedic Partners.
